title: ulmus glabra f. glabrata
intro: Ulmus glabra f. glabrata, also known as wych elm, is a subspecies of elm tree native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bark, leaves, and flowers of Ulmus glabra f. glabrata have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, due to their antiseptic and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of Ulmus glabra f. glabrata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
